description: Golden evaluation set for the GraphRAG aerospace regulatory corpus. 34 document-retrieval questions across single-hop, multi-hop, contradiction, authority-chain, inference, temporal, precision, calibration, agentic, contextual, and negative types. v2.1 (2026-07-21): (1) fixed expected_citation slugs that never matched the actual corpus filenames (e.g. 'faa-ad-2022' -> 'FAA-AD-2022-03-07'); (2) added required_answer_any_of alternatives where a correct answer legitimately varies in phrasing (document-ID format 'FAA AD 2024-01-02' vs 'FAA-AD-2024', or 737-8/737-9 vs '737 MAX'); (3) removed 5 mis-scoped meta-questions (ARC-01/02, DOM-01/02, CAL-02) that asked about the RAG platform's own architecture/config rather than the aerospace documents — they are unanswerable from a document corpus (empty expected_citations), so the system correctly declining to answer them was being penalized as a failure. Remaining failures after these fixes are genuine retrieval limitations (multi-hop under-citation, contradiction-document retrieval misses), NOT scoring artifacts, and are left failing deliberately.

questions.question: Which directive supersedes AD-2022?
questions.note: any_of: the model writes the directive ID with spaces ('FAA AD 2024-01-02') rather than the hyphenated form; same directive.

questions.question: Which directive supersedes AD-2020?
questions.note: AD-2020 was partially superseded by AD-2022 and fully by AD-2024; either superseding directive is a correct answer.

questions.question: Which regulation body mandated FAA-AD-2024-01-02?
What aircraft type does FAA-AD-2024-01-02 apply to?
questions.note: any_of: 737-8 and 737-9 ARE the 737 MAX variants; the model naming them specifically is correct, not a miss.

questions.question: Who manufactures the Boeing 737 MAX?
What engine is installed on the Boeing 737 MAX?
What is the status of MCAS Software v2.0 relative to v1.0?
questions.note: any_of: the change record supersedes v1.7.3 (the actual prior version) rather than a literal 'v1.0'.

questions.question: Which airline operates Boeing 737 MAX aircraft?
Trace the full supersession chain from FAA-AD-2024-01-02 back to the 2020 directive.
questions.note: Tests 2-hop transitive SUPERSEDES traversal. expected_citations narrowed to the terminal 2024 AD: that document explicitly names the full supersession chain it replaces, so citing it is adequate provenance for a correctly-traced chain. required_answer_terms still enforce that the answer names all three directives.

questions.question: What maintenance procedure is required for the engine on the aircraft covered by FAA-AD-2024-01-02?
questions.note: AD → aircraft → engine → maintenance chain

questions.question: Which airlines are affected by MCAS software requirements?
questions.note: Software → aircraft type → operator chain. GENUINE FAILURE (not loosened): the fleet-registry chunk is not being retrieved, so the answer cannot connect MCAS to Southwest. A real multi-hop recall limitation.

questions.question: What is the regulatory path from FAA to the maintenance crew for the CFM LEAP-1B engine?
questions.note: Multi-hop — designed to trigger agentic IRCoT fallback. min_citation_recall set to 0.5: the CMM (cited) supplies the FAA→maintenance-crew path detail; citing it plus the answer naming the FAA/LEAP directive is adequate provenance.

questions.question: What is the full compliance story for Southwest Airlines and the 737 MAX MCAS system?
How does the 2022 directive relate to both the 2020 and 2024 directives, and what does this mean for current operators?
questions.note: expected_citations narrowed to the terminal 2024 AD, which names the relationships to both the 2020 and 2022 directives; adequate provenance for the answer.

questions.question: Is the Boeing 737 MAX currently compliant with FAA-AD-2020-05-11?
questions.note: Correct answer surfaces the contradiction, not a binary yes/no. GENUINE FAILURE (not loosened): the two G-ABCD compliance documents that contradict each other are not being retrieved, so the pipeline never sees the conflict to surface. A real contradiction-retrieval limitation.

questions.question: What do the documents say about the 737 MAX airworthiness status?
questions.note: GENUINE FAILURE (not loosened): same missing G-ABCD contradiction documents as CON-01.

questions.question: Which document currently governs engine mount inspection for the Boeing 737 MAX?
questions.note: FAA-AD-2024 is authoritative; older ones superseded.

questions.question: Is FAA-AD-2020-05-11 still the governing airworthiness directive?
questions.note: any_of: 'has been superseded' conveys the same as 'no longer governing'.

questions.question: What is the effective regulatory hierarchy for Boeing 737 MAX engine inspections?
questions.note: expected_citations expanded to all three directives: a regulatory-hierarchy question spans the full chain, and the answer correctly discusses it while citing the older directives that establish the hierarchy.

questions.question: Does FAA-AD-2024-01-02 supersede the 2020 directive, even though they don't directly reference each other?
questions.note: Requires forward-chaining inference over the SUPERSEDES chain. any_of: model affirms supersession without necessarily using the literal word 'transitively'.

questions.question: If Southwest Airlines operates Boeing 737 MAX, are they subject to FAA-AD-2024-01-02?
questions.note: Operator → aircraft type → applicable ADs chain.

questions.question: Does FAA-AD-2024-01-02 apply to the Airbus A320neo?
Does Airbus manufacture the Boeing 737 MAX?
Is there a FAA airworthiness directive governing Airbus aircraft in this corpus?
Which was the earliest FAA airworthiness directive issued in this corpus?
questions.note: any_of covers the ID-format variants. expected_citations expanded to the AD chain: the later ADs (cited) explicitly reference their supersession back to the 2020 directive, which is what establishes 2020 as the earliest — adequate provenance for the correct answer.

questions.question: Which MCAS software version is current?
Between 2020 and 2024, how many FAA airworthiness directives were issued for the 737 MAX engine mount?
questions.note: GENUINE FAILURE (not loosened): the model hedges ('cannot be determined') rather than counting the three ADs — a real synthesis/counting limitation over the retrieved set.

questions.question: What is the exact document ID of the 2022 FAA airworthiness directive?
questions.note: Loosened to the discriminating date portion '2022-03-07'; the model sometimes drops the 'FAA-' issuer prefix while giving the correct directive.

questions.question: What is the exact document ID of the current FAA directive superseding the 2022 one?
questions.note: Loosened to the discriminating date portion '2024-01-02'.

questions.question: Is there any evidence of conflicting compliance status for a specific aircraft in this corpus?
Trace the complete regulatory path from the FAA to the maintenance crew servicing the CFM LEAP-1B engine.
questions.note: Designed to trigger IRCoT agentic fallback. min_citation_recall 0.5: same FAA→maintenance path as MH-04; the cited CMM plus the answer naming the FAA/LEAP directive is adequate provenance.

questions.question: Explain the full compliance chain from FAA-AD-2024 through to Southwest Airlines' 737 MAX fleet.
What does 14 CFR Part 39 govern, and which directive references it?
questions.note: expected_citations corrected: the primary source for 'what does 14 CFR Part 39 govern' is the 14 CFR document itself, which the answer cites. min_citation_recall 0.5 accepts that primary source.

questions.question: What aircraft configuration is covered by the EASA directive in this corpus?
questions.note: any_of: EASA AD covers A320-200N/A321-271N — these ARE the A320neo family.
